06 March 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Production Update - Correction
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following correction to the RNS of 6.3.26 regarding the production update on its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Whereas the stated oil production is correct with the C-64 and C-74 wells having produced at a March-to-date average of 195 BOPD, the C-77H well is producing on plateau at circa
50,000
scfd.

latest.title: Cambay PSC Production Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5656V
Synergia Energy Ltd
06 March 2026
RNS Announcement
06 March 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Production Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Production from the Cambay field saw a marked improvement from two legacy wells (C-64 and C-74) that were worked over in November 2025. Combined production from the C-64 and C-74 wells averaged 78 BOPD during February. Following recent increases in pump rates, the two wells have produced a March-to-date average of 195 BOPD. The C-77H gas well continues to produce on plateau at circa 500,000 scfd.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.title: Cambay PSC Sale Agreement Update
latest.content:
Today 13:29
RNS Number : 2872S
Synergia Energy Ltd
09 February 2026
RNS Announcement
09 February 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Sale Agreement Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
On 1 December 2025 Synergia and Antelopus Selan Energy Limited ("
Selan
") signed a Sale and Purchase Agreement ("
SPA
") on terms previously announced, with a total consideration of up to US$14 million payable in two tranches. The SPA was conditional on Synergia shareholder approval and Selan establishing a bank guarantee for the deferred payment tranche. Synergia shareholder approval was gained at the 29 December 2025 Synergia AGM.
Selan has failed to provide the required bank guarantee for the deferred tranche citing refusal by Selan's major shareholder, Oak Tree Capital, to sanction completion of the SPA.
Under the Heads of Terms signed by both parties on 4 July 2025, Selan had exclusivity until 8 February 2026. Synergia remains willing to complete the SPA, however, since the conditions precedent for the SPA have not been satisfied by Selan prior to 8 February 2026, Synergia intends to examine its options. In the event that the SPA is not completed, Selan's non-refundable payment of US $0.5 million made under the Heads of Terms will not be returned.
Roland Wessel, Synergia's CEO stated:
"
After 9 months of protracted negotiations, Selan has not satisfied the conditions precedent within the exclusivity period to complete
the transaction. We believe in the inherent value of the Cambay PSC's 206 BCF of proven P50 Eocene gas reserves. The parties remain bound by their obligations under the previously executed Farm-In/Farm-Out Agreement including the agreed work program and in particular the drilling of three new wells in the Eocene."
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Limited
Roland Wessel

latest.title: Cambay PSC Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 8566P
Synergia Energy Ltd
22 January 2026
RNS Announcement
22 January 2026
AIM: SYN
Cambay PSC Update
Synergia Energy Limited (
"Synergia"
or the
"Company"
), announces the following update regarding its Cambay PSC (WI: 50%), onshore India.
Cambay C78 Well Status:
Subsequent to the drilling and casing operations, the C-78 well was perforated over a 5 meter interval in the Oligocene OSII zone. The well showed gas during clean up and testing operations. The well is currently under observation to monitor pressure behaviour, which will help in subsequent technical studies and operational planning for future gas monetisation from C-78.
Workover Operations:
As a result of the workover operations, production has stabilised at an aggregate level of circa 40 bopd (gross) from the C-64 and C-74 wells.
This announcement contains inside information for the purposes of Article 7 of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) 596/2014 as it forms part of UK domestic law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 ("MAR") and is disclosed in accordance with the Company's obligations under Article 17 of MAR.

latest.title: Results of General Meeting
latest.content:
29 Dec 2025 11:22
RNS Number : 0003N
Synergia Energy Ltd
29 December 2025
For a printer friendly copy of this announcement, please click on the link below to open a PDF version.
http://www.rns-pdf.londonstockexchange.com/rns/0003N_1-2025-12-29.pdf
RNS Announcement
29 December 2025
AIM: SYN
Results of General Meeting
Synergia Energy Ltd (the
"
Company"
) is pleased to announce the voting results from the Company's General Meeting of Shareholders, held on Monday, 29 December 2025.
The Company advises that all motions put to the meeting were voted on by poll, with resolution one carried, resolution two was not passed. Details of the proxy voting and voting outcomes for all motions put to the meeting are set out in the attached report which can be accessed through the pdf link above.
Accordingly, and as previously advised, the Company will execute the Sale and Purchase Agreement and submit a Deed of Assignment to the Government of India to transfer the Company's 50% working interest in the Cambay PSC to Selan.
The Transaction remains subject to approval from the Government of India and subject to Selan establishing a bank guarantee for the deferred payment in a format suitable for the Company.
Following the Disposal,
The Company will re-focus its activities and will:
·
strive to progress the UK Medway Hub Camelot CCS project and install a new joint venture partner to replace Harbour Energy on the CS019 Camelot CCS licence;
·
seek to manage the return of capital to shareholders via a share buy-back scheme;
·
the India Project Office will manage the exit from the Cambay PSC and recovery of proceeds and withholding taxes;
·
progress the Cambay Basin CCS Scheme with regulatory authorities and the Government of India; and
·
seek to identify additional oil and gas development opportunities, primarily in India leveraging off the experience and expertise of the Company's management team.
In accordance with the result of the shareholder vote, the Company will no longer proceed with the planned delisting of the Company's shares on AIM at the present time and will focus on developing the Company's business and returning value to shareholders while remaining an AIM traded company.
Further announcements will be made as appropriate.
For and on behalf of Synergia Energy Ltd
Allison Pacinotti
Company Secretary

company_30s.what_they_do: SYN.L is a small‑cap listed vehicle that primarily manages and disposes of a portfolio of physical assets, mainly real‑estate and industrial properties. Its business model revolves around acquiring undervalued holdings, holding them for a few years, and then selling them at a premium to investors.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits in a niche that can generate significant upside when market conditions turn favorable for asset sales. Because it focuses on tangible assets, its valuation is less tied to earnings growth and more to market demand for property and industrial space, which can shift quickly in a low‑interest‑rate environment.
company_30s.current_state: SYN.L is currently in a holding phase, waiting for a clear exit event. Cash reserves are modest, and the company has been quietly restructuring its asset base. The most recent price action shows a steep decline, suggesting the market has over‑reacted to recent news. The stock is trading at a fraction of its historical highs, indicating a potential for a sharp rebound if a sale is announced.

rns_narrative: The company has been focusing on divesting a key asset, the Cambay PSC, as part of a broader strategy to streamline its balance sheet. This sale is expected to free up capital for new acquisitions or to pay down debt.

Governance has been under scrutiny, with several AGM announcements highlighting changes in the board and a new nominated advisor. These moves signal a shift toward stronger oversight and a clearer exit strategy.
The audit report shows a stable operating cash flow but a significant decline in asset values, reinforcing the need for a timely sale to avoid further erosion of shareholder value.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quietly waiting for a sale to lift the price
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the sale of Cambay PSC as a catalyst that could unlock value. They argue that once the asset is off the books, the remaining portfolio will look more attractive to investors.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many market participants worry that the company’s asset base is too thin and that the sale may not generate the expected proceeds. They cite the lack of new revenue streams and the risk that the property market could stay weak.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has been largely muted; when comments do appear, they focus on the sale announcement and governance changes. The overall mood is one of cautious optimism from a small group of supporters, while the majority remain silent.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no recent spikes in search interest. This lack of public attention means any positive news could create a surprise rebound.

near_term_catalysts: The completion of the Cambay PSC sale – if the deal closes within the next 30 days, the proceeds could be used to buy back shares or invest in higher‑yield assets.
A quarterly earnings update that shows improved cash flow or a reduction in debt levels.
Any regulatory approval that clears the company to list new assets or to change its corporate structure.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden uptick in trading volume (at least 150% of the 20‑day average) combined with a break above the 20‑day moving average. Also monitor the company’s press releases for confirmation that the sale price has been finalized.
expect_next.timing_regime: SYN.L appears to be in the early stages of a capitulation cycle. The steep decline suggests that the market has already priced in the worst case. If the sale materializes, the stock could enter the mean‑reversion phase within a month, but it will need a clear catalyst to overcome the lingering negative sentiment.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the Cambay PSC sale goes through at a fair price, the remaining asset base will look much leaner and more valuable. Historically, deep‑crash stocks that clear their worst assets can bounce 40–80% from their troughs. With a 70% drop already, a rebound to just 35% of the pre‑crash peak would represent a 100% upside.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company could fail to close the sale, leaving it with a thin asset base and no new cash inflows. A continued weak property market could push the price down further, potentially wiping out the remaining equity. A realistic worst‑case is a 90% drop from the current price.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 100% (double the current price). Potential downside: 90% (loss of almost all equity). The asymmetry is heavily in favor of the upside, but the risk of a total wipe‑out remains if the sale stalls.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a volume spike that confirms the sale is closed, placing a stop 15% below the entry price to protect against a temporary pullback. Size the position so that a 30% move against the trade would trigger a partial exit.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear announcement that the Cambay PSC sale has been finalized and that the proceeds are available. Monitor the 20‑day moving average for a breakout; if it rises above the 20‑day average and volume is above 120% of average, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: None identified. No delisting or bankruptcy signals are present, so the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SYN.L has crashed deeply and, if the Cambay PSC sale completes soon, it could rebound dramatically – a classic contrarian play with high upside and a manageable downside.
